findings.description: yaml.load(f) without SafeLoader deserializes untrusted config into arbitrary Python objects, enabling remote code execution via YAML tags (e.g. !!python/object/apply:os.system); fix with yaml.safe_load (CWE-502, OWASP A08).
The isinstance(data, dict) guard runs after yaml.load, so it provides no protection against the deserialization RCE because the payload executes during loading, not after.

config_path is opened and deserialized directly, so if any caller ever supplies user-influenced input as the path this becomes arbitrary-file-read into the yaml.load RCE chain (CWE-22 adjacency); low confidence since path is app-controlled in current scope.

Precedence is inverted versus the documented contract: docstring promises env > file, but the later .update(self._read_file()) lets the file override env vars, which weakens env-based security hardening overrides; primarily a logic bug for the sibling critic.
